history_preview.subject: Enron reports recurring earnings growth in 1997 and sets up 1998 growth platform
history_preview.snippet: Archived year-end earnings release describing 1997 results and the company's 1998 growth posture.

history_preview.subject: Enron reports second quarter 1998 earnings of $0.42 per share
history_preview.snippet: Archived second-quarter 1998 earnings release describing continued wholesale growth and contract execution.

history_preview.subject: Enron reports third quarter 1998 earnings and raises the annual dividend
history_preview.snippet: Archived third-quarter 1998 earnings release covering wholesale growth, retail expansion, and dividend increase.

history_preview.subject: FY1998 selected financial data
history_preview.snippet: Operating revenue was $31.26B, net income was $703M, total assets were $29.35B, shareholders' equity was $7.05B, and diluted EPS was $1.01.

history_preview.subject: Enron reports increased earnings results for the first quarter of 1999
history_preview.snippet: Archived first-quarter 1999 earnings release describing wholesale strength, retail contracting, and growing credit-market exposure.

history_preview.subject: FY1999 selected financial data
history_preview.snippet: Operating revenue was $40.11B, net income was $893M, total assets were $33.38B, shareholders' equity was $9.57B, and diluted EPS was $1.10.

history_preview.subject: Q4 1999 annual earnings release
history_preview.snippet: Enron reported full-year 1999 revenues of $40.0B, net income of $957M before nonrecurring items, annual diluted EPS of $1.18, and fourth-quarter diluted EPS of $0.31.

history_preview.subject: Enron launched its first online emissions allowance auction
history_preview.snippet: Enron said EnronOnline would host the first online sulfur dioxide emissions allowance auction and planned to run the auctions monthly.

history_preview.subject: Enron files its Form 10-K for fiscal year 1999
history_preview.snippet: Annual report and Form 10-K filing covering the fiscal year ended December 31, 1999.

history_preview.subject: Enron signed a $610M long-term electricity agreement with IBM
history_preview.snippet: Enron Energy Services said it would supply or procure electricity for IBM under a ten-year agreement valued at $610M.

history_preview.subject: Q1 2000 earnings release
history_preview.snippet: Enron reported first-quarter revenues of $13.1B, net income of $338M, and diluted EPS of $0.40.

history_preview.subject: Enron announced a cash offer for MG plc
history_preview.snippet: Enron said its recommended cash offer valued MG plc at $446M and would extend Enron's business model into metals.

history_preview.subject: EnronOnline passed $50B of transaction value in 2000
history_preview.snippet: Enron said EnronOnline had exceeded $50B of transaction value in calendar 2000 and had already beaten its yearly projection.

history_preview.subject: Enron and Blockbuster announced a 20-year on-demand service deal
history_preview.snippet: Enron Broadband Services and Blockbuster announced a 20-year exclusive agreement to launch an entertainment-on-demand service.

history_preview.subject: Q2 2000 earnings release
history_preview.snippet: Enron reported second-quarter revenues of $16.9B, net income of $289M, and diluted EPS of $0.34.

history_preview.subject: Enron launched Clickpaper.com for the forest products market
history_preview.snippet: Enron Industrial Markets launched Clickpaper.com as an Internet transaction system for pulp, paper, and wood commodities.

history_preview.subject: Q3 2000 earnings release
history_preview.snippet: Enron reported third-quarter revenues of $30.0B, net income of $292M, and diluted EPS of $0.34.

history_preview.subject: Fitch baseline senior debt rating
history_preview.snippet: Enron's senior debt was listed at BBB+ in the 2000 financial highlights.

history_preview.subject: FY2000 Form 10-K
history_preview.snippet: Operating revenue was $100.79B, net income was $979M, total assets were $65.50B, shareholders' equity was $11.47B, and diluted EPS was $1.12.

history_preview.subject: Moody's baseline senior debt rating
history_preview.snippet: Enron's senior debt was listed at Baa1 in the 2000 financial highlights.

history_preview.subject: S&P baseline senior debt rating
history_preview.snippet: Enron's senior debt was listed at BBB+ in the 2000 financial highlights.

history_preview.subject: Q4 2000 annual earnings release
history_preview.snippet: Enron publicly reported recurring annual diluted EPS of $1.47 for 2000 and fourth-quarter diluted EPS of $0.41.

history_preview.subject: PG&E files for Chapter 11 protection
history_preview.snippet: SEC filing record documenting PG&E's April 6, 2001 bankruptcy filing.

history_preview.subject: PG&E entered Chapter 11
history_preview.snippet: PG&E's Chapter 11 filing made California power-counterparty risk a public fact before later Enron branch points tied to power trading and structured deals.

history_preview.subject: Q1 2001 earnings release
history_preview.snippet: Enron publicly reported first-quarter diluted EPS of $0.47, revenues of $50.1B, and net income of $406M.

history_preview.subject: Vice chairman Cliff Baxter resigned
history_preview.snippet: Enron's archived 2001 press chronology records Cliff Baxter's resignation on May 2, 2001.

history_preview.subject: FERC imposed Western mitigation and opened the refund path
history_preview.snippet: The June 19, 2001 FERC action turned California power-market conduct and refund exposure into a concrete public regulatory fact.

history_preview.subject: FERC imposed mitigation and opened the California refund path
history_preview.snippet: Public reporting on the June 19, 2001 FERC action put Enron and other sellers on notice that California crisis sales would face a refund and mitigation regime.

history_preview.subject: Q2 2001 earnings release
history_preview.snippet: Enron publicly reported second-quarter diluted EPS of $0.45 and net income of $404M.

history_preview.subject: Jeffrey Skilling resigns as chief executive officer
history_preview.snippet: Public resignation record that marks the executive transition during Enron's disclosure spiral.

history_preview.subject: Jeff Skilling resigned as chief executive
history_preview.snippet: Enron announced Skilling's resignation for personal reasons and said Kenneth Lay would resume the president and chief executive role.

history_preview.snippet: Mark
